name: Pork Chops with White Beans
recipeIngredient: 3 cups dry white beans, soaked overnight
recipeInstructions:
1. Season the pork chops with Creole spice.
2. Heat oil over medium-high heat in a Dutch oven or heavy, deep pot.
3. Brown the pork chops on both sides; remove to a plate.
4. Reduce the heat. Add celery, onions, green pepper, and garlic. Sweat until tender.
5. Stir in beans, tomatoes, bay leaves, and stock. Return pork chops to the pot.
6. Bring to a boil, cover, reduce heat, and simmer on very low heat for several hours,
or until beans are very tender. Adjust seasonings.
Notes: Mild chicken, beef, veggie stock, or water can be used instead of stock.
